frameworks.name: internal-signal decoding from large-scale recordings
frameworks.one_line: reference neural activity to itself to detect covert decision signals across regions

frameworks.name: low-dimensional decision state dynamics
frameworks.one_line: continuously evolving decision variable shared across many brain regions

frameworks.name: discrete brain-state transition at commitment
frameworks.one_line: covert commitment time triggers a global formation-to-post-commitment switch

frameworks.name: perceptual decision-making
frameworks.one_line: rats performing a perceptual choice task as the behavioral substrate

core_question: How are covert decision-related internal signals coordinated across brain regions during perceptual decision-making?
key_assumptions: decision state can be decoded continuously from each region's population activity
commitment time is a covert event decodable from primary motor cortex
simultaneous multi-region recordings reveal organism-internal signals
manipulations: simultaneous recording across up to 20 brain regions
decoding decision state separately per region
decoding commitment time from primary motor cortex activity
key_findings: regional decision-state estimates show large co-fluctuations across the brain
co-fluctuations are dominated by a single state variable, not parallel computations
decoded commitment time coincides with a brain-wide state change
pre- and post-commitment states differ in choice-predictive dynamics and inter-region correlations
interpretations: a single decision-making computation, not multiple parallel ones, operates during this period
commitment marks a coordinated transition from decision formation to a post-commitment regime
